name: Archaeological Cultural Landscape of Carnuntum
location: Lower Austria, between Petronell-Carnuntum and Bad Deutsch-Altenburg, Danube Limes
description_summary: Major Roman frontier settlement including legionary fortress, canabae, civilian town, amphitheatres, and extensive burial grounds. Part of the UNESCO Danube Limes World Heritage. Over 10 km² of archaeological landscape with over 90% still unexcavated and preserved underground.
assessment_method: Austrian Federal Monuments Authority criteria: historical, artistic, general cultural significance
management_context: Legally listed monument (2022). Archaeological park with museum infrastructure. Ongoing non-invasive prospection (LBI ArchPro).
key_dates: 1st century CE - Roman military camp established
2021 - Danube Limes inscribed as UNESCO WH
integrity_notes: High integrity due to 90%+ remaining unexcavated. Agricultural use has been largely non-destructive. Some areas affected by historical stone robbing.
comparators: Other Danube Limes sites; comparable to Vindolanda (UK), Xanten (Germany)
unique_features: One of the best-preserved Roman frontier landscapes in Europe; extensive use of non-invasive prospection technologies

name: Alpine Landscape at Pitschenbergalm
location: Upper Austria, Alpine region, mountain pasture at high altitude
description_summary: High-altitude mountain pasture with multi-period archaeological remains including prehistoric pastoral activity, charcoal production, and medieval alpine farming. Discovered through modern survey methods including LiDAR.
values_identified: General cultural significance (traditional alpine pastoral culture)
assessment_method: Austrian Federal Monuments Authority: historical significance + general cultural significance evaluation
threats: Climate change effects on alpine environments
management_context: Evaluated as part of in-house Federal Monuments Authority project (2020). Expert report on significance produced.
key_dates: Prehistoric to medieval - phases of use
integrity_notes: Relatively well preserved due to remote alpine location. Minimal modern disturbance.
comparators: Other alpine archaeological sites in Austria and Switzerland
unique_features: Demonstrates significance assessment of landscape-scale alpine archaeology; LiDAR discovery

name: Archaeological Landscape in Kurese
description_summary: Complex archaeological landscape with interconnected settlement remains, burial sites, and field systems spanning multiple periods. Demonstrates challenges of protecting archaeological landscapes in rural Estonia.
values_identified: Cultural landscape value (field systems, land use patterns)
assessment_method: Estonian Heritage Conservation Act criteria; National Heritage Board evaluation
management_context: National Heritage Board protection process. Challenges with designating sites as monuments due to restrictions on landowners.
key_dates: Iron Age through medieval periods of use
integrity_notes: Variable - some areas well preserved under forest, others affected by modern agriculture
unique_features: Illustrates tension between heritage protection and forest/land management in Baltic context

name: Mapping Archaeologically-sensitive Areas for Municipalities
description_summary: Methodology for mapping archaeologically sensitive areas to integrate into municipal comprehensive plans. Uses predictive modeling and known site data to create planning tools for municipalities.
assessment_method: Predictive GIS-based modeling combined with known site data
threats: Lack of archaeological data in planning
management_context: Finnish Heritage Agency initiative to integrate archaeology into spatial planning at municipal level.
comparators: Similar GIS-based predictive approaches in Netherlands, UK, Scandinavia
unique_features: Proactive approach to heritage management through spatial planning integration; landscape-scale assessment

name: Raseborg Castle
description_summary: Medieval castle ruin with multiple construction phases, reflecting changing political and military conditions in the medieval Baltic region. Includes castle structures, surrounding settlement remains, and landscape context.
assessment_method: Finnish Heritage Agency scoring system: cultural-historic significance, representativeness, rarity, information value, landscape/environmental value. Scale 0-2 or 1-3.
management_context: Finnish Heritage Agency protected site. Scoring system applied with quantified evaluation criteria.
integrity_notes: Castle ruins in variable condition. Scored high (3) on cultural-historic significance.
unique_features: Demonstrates Finnish quantitative scoring approach to significance assessment

name: The Vrouw Maria Wreck
location: Archipelago National Park, southwestern Finland, Baltic Sea
description_summary: Dutch merchant vessel that sank in 1771 while carrying art collection purchased by Catherine the Great of Russia. Remarkably well-preserved wreck at approximately 41 meters depth in cold Baltic waters. One of the most significant underwater archaeological sites in Finland.
assessment_method: Finnish three-level classification for wrecks; multi-criteria evaluation including cultural-historic significance, representativeness, rarity, information potential
management_context: Protected under Antiquities Act. Located in National Park. Subject of international media attention and political discussion about possible salvage.
integrity_notes: Exceptionally well preserved in cold Baltic conditions. Cargo potentially intact.
unique_features: Demonstrates underwater heritage significance assessment; intersection of art history and maritime archaeology

name: Protection of Tumuli in Hungary
description_summary: National-scale effort to document and protect burial mounds (tumuli/kurgans) across the Hungarian landscape. Thousands of tumuli from various periods face threats from agriculture and development. Represents a collection-level heritage management challenge.
values_identified: Landscape value (defining feature of Hungarian plains)
assessment_method: Hungarian heritage protection criteria; inventory and mapping
management_context: National legislation (1711 threshold). Ongoing inventory effort. Challenge of protecting thousands of dispersed features.
key_dates: Bronze Age through Migration period - construction
integrity_notes: Highly variable - many reduced by agriculture; some well preserved in pastureland
comparators: Kurgans in Ukraine, Russia; barrows in UK, Netherlands, Scandinavia
unique_features: Collection-level management challenge; landscape-scale heritage protection; agricultural landscape context

name: Pilis Mountains and Szentendre Island Programme
location: Pilis Mountains and Szentendre Island, near Budapest, Hungary
description_summary: Large-scale regional research programme covering the Pilis Mountains and Szentendre Island area near Budapest. Integrates archaeological survey, GIS mapping, historical research, and landscape analysis across multiple periods. Demonstrates systematic regional approach to significance assessment.
assessment_method: Regional systematic survey with GIS; multi-criteria landscape assessment
management_context: University-led research programme (Central European University). Combines academic research with heritage management objectives.
key_dates: Prehistoric through Ottoman - periods of occupation
integrity_notes: Variable across the region. Forest cover has protected some areas; others affected by modern development.
comparators: Other regional landscape programmes in Europe
unique_features: Largest case study (35K chars); demonstrates regional-scale systematic approach; academic-management partnership

name: Hveravellir
location: Northern Iceland, highland interior between Langjökull and Hofsjökull glaciers
description_summary: Geothermal area in the Icelandic highlands used as a waystation on historic travel routes across the interior. Contains remains of shelters, sheep gathering structures, and outlaw hideouts. Combines natural heritage (hot springs) with cultural heritage (travel routes, saga connections).
values_identified: Historical significance (medieval travel routes, saga connections)
Scientific value (archaeological remains in extreme environment)
Intangible heritage value (saga traditions, outlaw stories)
assessment_method: Icelandic scoring system with 10 evaluation areas; Cultural Heritage Agency of Iceland evaluation
management_context: Cultural Heritage Agency of Iceland. Intersection of natural and cultural heritage management. Tourism management challenges.
key_dates: Viking Age onwards - use as travel route
integrity_notes: Some remains well preserved in highland conditions. Tourism infrastructure has impacted some areas.
comparators: Other Icelandic highland sites; comparable geothermal heritage sites globally
unique_features: Nature-culture interface; extreme environment archaeology; saga tradition connections; outlaw heritage

name: Þjórsárdalur Cultural Landscape
description_summary: Valley landscape with remains of Viking Age and medieval farmsteads abandoned after the 1104 Hekla volcanic eruption. Preserved under volcanic tephra. Includes the reconstructed Stöng farmstead, one of the best-known Viking Age buildings.
values_identified: Landscape value (volcanic landscape, abandoned settlement pattern)
assessment_method: Cultural landscape significance assessment by Cultural Heritage Agency of Iceland
management_context: Protected cultural landscape. Stöng reconstruction as public interpretation site.
integrity_notes: Exceptional preservation under volcanic tephra. Some areas disturbed by river activity and erosion.
comparators: Pompeii (volcanic preservation); other Icelandic settlement sites
unique_features: Volcanic preservation creating "frozen in time" moment; connection between natural disaster and cultural heritage

name: Waterford City Viking Triangle
description_summary: Historic urban core of Waterford, Ireland's oldest city, with continuous occupation from Viking foundation. Archaeological excavations revealed extensive Viking and medieval remains. Now a heritage quarter integrating archaeology into urban development and tourism.
values_identified: Historical significance (oldest Irish city, Viking foundation)
assessment_method: Irish National Monuments legislation; archaeological significance criteria within development control
management_context: National Monuments Act protection. Integration of heritage into urban regeneration through Viking Triangle Heritage Quarter.
integrity_notes: Deeply stratified urban deposits. Some areas excavated; extensive deposits remain in situ beneath modern city.
unique_features: Successful integration of archaeology into urban regeneration; development-led archaeology in living city

name: Tuba-Zangariyye Dolmen Field
location: Korazim Plateau, northern Israel, between Sea of Galilee and Hula Valley
description_summary: Dense concentration of 65+ dolmens and 20+ tumuli on the basalt Korazim Plateau. Part of a larger regional megalithic landscape. Threatened by residential development. Significance assessment led to revised development plan incorporating dolmen preservation.
values_identified: Rarity (one of last remaining intact dolmen concentrations)
Biblical/intangible value (possible references in Deuteronomy and Mark)
assessment_method: Context-Based Significance Assessment (CBSA) model (Alef, 2021). Five-step process: identify elements/contexts → articulate significance → contextualize → compare → summarize.
management_context: Israel Antiquities Authority. Shift from salvage-excavation approach to preservation-in-situ. CBSA used to argue for plan revision.
integrity_notes: Well-preserved concentration, though dozens of dolmens destroyed in wider Korazim Plateau. Some covering stones weigh up to 15 tons.
comparators: Korazim Plateau wider dolmen field; Golan Heights megalithic sites; southern Syria megalithic field
unique_features: Demonstrates CBSA methodology in practice; shift from salvage to preservation; biblical landscape connections

name: Leithon
location: Leiderdorp, near The Hague and Leiden, western Netherlands
description_summary: Remains of an early medieval settlement along the former Oude Rijn river course. Scheduled as national archaeological monument. Site includes settlement remains, wooden/stone constructions, human remains, and animal bones. Previously used as tennis courts.
values_identified: Rarity (well-preserved early medieval site in urban context)
assessment_method: Dutch rating system: intrinsic (scientific) value, physical quality, absolute quality. National scheduling criteria.
management_context: Scheduled national archaeological monument. Cultural Heritage Agency of the Netherlands oversight.
integrity_notes: Archaeological deposits preserved beneath modern surface (tennis courts). Depth provides some protection.
comparators: Other Rhine delta early medieval settlements; Dorestad
unique_features: Demonstrates Dutch intrinsic-value-based assessment system; preservation under modern urban surface

name: Prehistoric Barrows as Archive for Future Research
description_summary: Policy-level discussion of prehistoric barrows as repositories of future scientific knowledge. Argues for preservation as archives that will yield information through future technologies not yet available.
values_identified: Archival value (information stored for future retrieval)
assessment_method: Future research value assessment; Dutch heritage management framework
threats: Excavation that removes future research potential
management_context: Cultural Heritage Agency of the Netherlands. Policy argument for preservation over excavation.
integrity_notes: Variable. Many barrows leveled by agriculture; surviving examples increasingly rare.
comparators: UK round barrows; Scandinavian burial mounds; Hungarian tumuli (case 7.7)
unique_features: Explicitly frames archaeological sites as archives for future research; challenges excavation-first approach

name: Hillfort Zamczysko (The Burgstall)
description_summary: Early medieval hillfort with preserved ramparts and settlement remains. Demonstrates Polish approach to archaeological site protection and significance evaluation within the national monument system.
assessment_method: Polish National Institute of Cultural Heritage criteria; Voivodship Monuments Preservation Officer evaluation
management_context: Protected under Polish Act on Monument Protection. Voivodship-level management.
integrity_notes: Rampart structure preserved. Some disturbance from historical and illegal activities.
comparators: Other early medieval hillforts in Poland and Central Europe
unique_features: Demonstrates Polish heritage protection system; landscape-prominent archaeological feature

name: Church of St Nicholas and the Romanesque Cellar, Gdańsk
site_type: Urban religious / architectural site with buried remains
description_summary: Medieval church with Romanesque-period cellar discovered beneath. Complex urban archaeological site where above-ground medieval architecture overlies earlier buried remains. Demonstrates challenges of managing multi-period urban heritage.
values_identified: Symbolic value (Gdańsk identity, post-war reconstruction context)
assessment_method: Polish heritage protection criteria; architectural and archaeological combined assessment
management_context: Listed monument. Complex interplay between above-ground and below-ground heritage management.
key_dates: WWII - Destruction and reconstruction of Gdańsk
integrity_notes: Romanesque cellar well preserved beneath later construction. Urban context both protects and threatens.
comparators: Other Hanseatic city archaeological sites; York, Lübeck
unique_features: Above-ground + below-ground heritage interplay; post-war urban reconstruction context

name: Dumbarton Rock
location: Dumbarton, west-central Scotland, confluence of River Leven and Clyde
description_summary: Dramatic volcanic rock formation that served as capital of the ancient kingdom of Strathclyde (Alt Clut). Multiple heritage designations. Unique case where community participation (rock climbers) led to expanding the official Statement of Significance to include climbing heritage.
values_identified: Landscape/scenic value (dramatic volcanic rock on river)
assessment_method: Historic Environment Scotland Statement of Significance format: chronological summary, thematic discussions, social overview, assessment of significance with period-based rankings.
management_context: Historic Environment Scotland site in care. Multiple legal designations. Community co-production of Statement of Significance.
integrity_notes: Rock itself permanent; built structures in various states. Archaeological deposits partially investigated.
comparators: Edinburgh Castle Rock; Stirling Castle; other Scottish fortress-rocks
unique_features: Community co-production of significance; rock climbing as heritage value; nature-culture integration; first community-contributed Statement of Significance
